title: fix: repair polly assistant workflow
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt:
Polly's been failing on every run. This gets it working again.

- Pins claude-code-router to v2.0.0
- Switches models to gpt-5.6-sol, minimax, and perplexity
- Adds a small shim so request bodies come out in the format the API expects

gist.headline: Polly finds her footing
gist.blurb: Polly’s workshop tools were put back in the right drawers. The assistant can once again buzz through repository tasks without tripping over request formats.
gist.summary: Repaired the Repo Polly GitHub Actions workflow by pinning claude-code-router to v2.0.0, switching its model set to gpt-5.6-sol, MiniMax, and Perplexity, and adding a request-body compatibility shim.
gist.impact: Repository maintainers regain a working Polly assistant workflow with API requests formatted for the current routing setup.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden-shed automation workshop where Polly, wearing a tiny mechanic tool belt, reconnects three labeled model cables to a friendly retro terminal while a small code-plant grows beside it; the visual metaphor is a request-format adapter making the workflow run smoothly again.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny mechanic tool belt, carefully plugging three pastel model cables into a chunky retro terminal through a small adapter labeled “API”; a lime green (#ecf874) code-plant blooms beside the terminal in a warm cream garden workshop. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle CRT glow, emotionally warm and inviting.
